Details: Removal of As(III), As(V), total arsenic, and arsenite from water by membrane separation processes can be very effective (25 to greater than 99 percent removal; 15 to greater than 99 percent removal; 58 to greater than 99 percent removal; and 26 to 93 percent removal, respectively). Reverse osmosis (RO) has been identified as a Best Available Technology for arsenic removal, while nanofiltration has also been used in some cases. RO is a pressure-driven membrane separation process capable of removing arsenic from water by means of particle size, dielectric characteristics, and hydrophilicity/ hydrophobicity. Some laboratory studies demonstrated effective As(V) removal by Micellar Enhanced Ultra-Filtration (MEUF) processes (55 to 96 percent removal) [2718]. Factors such as applied pressure on the membranes, feedwater temperature, influent arsenic concentration, and source water chemical composition, have strong influence on the efficiency of membrane processes.
